markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define schema version migrations and compatibility handling for persisted Tiptap/ProseMirror JSON. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: TiptapSchemaVersioning leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: TiptapSchemaVersioning is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 5f1b0939: schema_versioning.test.ts 5/5 - assertEditorSchema pass-through/migrate/refuse-newer/typed-unknown wired into RichDocumentView load. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: H2 schema fail-open (newer/unknown-schema docs editable + saveable; one save persists the ProseMirror-stripped doc)
evidence.fix: RichDocumentView fail-closed: schemaBlocked state -> editor mounted READ-ONLY (data-schema-blocked), Save disabled, onSave refuses with typed schemaSaveBlockedError; schemaMismatchError hint states the read-only/save-blocked contract
tests: RichDocumentView.test.tsx: fails CLOSED on a newer-schema document: read-only editor, save blocked, typed banner (incl. destructive-save negative: forced edit + Save click never reaches saveRichDocument)
backend_error.test.ts: builds a typed save-blocked error for schema-blocked documents

adversarial_review_v2.suites_observed: schema_versioning.test.ts 5/5 PASS in current tree (within vitest 62/468)

findings.title: Fail-open on schema mismatch: newer/unknown-schema documents stay editable and saveable -> silent data loss
findings.evidence: RichDocumentView.tsx:74-79 - when assertEditorSchema returns ok:false the view STILL setEditorContent(response.document.content_json) and leaves the editor editable with Save enabled. schema_versioning.ts:54-57 states a non-ok result "must be surfaced (MT-174) rather than loaded". ProseMirror (enableContentCheck default false) silently DROPS unknown node types on parse, so one Save persists a stripped document over the newer-schema head (versions table keeps history, head is degraded).
findings.fix: On ok:false: keep the typed banner, render read-only (editable:false) or a raw-JSON preview, and hard-disable Save until reload under a compatible editor. Add a regression test: load newer_than_editor doc -> editor readOnly -> save blocked.

findings.title: Migrated content is never persisted until a manual edit; migration ladder unexercised end-to-end
findings.evidence: RichDocumentView.tsx:74-75 loads assertion.content for compatibility "migrated" but isDirty stays false, so the migrated JSON + new schema_version are not saved; every load re-migrates. SCHEMA_MIGRATIONS is empty (schema_versioning.ts:46) and no integration test covers migrate->save->reload stamping.
findings.fix: After a successful migration either mark dirty with an explicit "migrated - save to upgrade" notice or auto-save a migration version; add an end-to-end test with a synthetic v0->v1 migration through RichDocumentView.

parity_gaps: ED-CORE-004 schema versioning: module PRESENT, consumer fail-open -> PARTIAL at the surface
missing_tests: load newer_than_editor schema -> editor is read-only and save is blocked
load unknown_version schema -> unknown nodes are NOT silently dropped into a saveable doc
migrated document -> save stamps current schema_version (end-to-end through RichDocumentView)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
